content.text: I have a deeply nested dictionary for configuration data. How can I flatten it into a single level while keeping track of the original keys?
What happens if one of the values in the dictionary is a list instead of a dict?

Currently, if a value is a list, it is treated as a non-dictionary leaf node and assigned directly to the flat dictionary under its original key. To handle lists specifically, you would need to add an extra check for `isinstance(val, list)` in the else block.
This looks like a robust solution! Thanks.
